description: Browser automation and artifacts remain local by default.
Repository must not contain committed secret-like data.
Runtime must not reuse existing browser profiles without approval.
Review artifacts, findings, quality signals, and reports remain local and evidence-path based by default.

MCP compatibility uses stdio by default and permits only explicit safe-profile loopback HTTP transport plus token-free client config and read-only capability policy metadata; socket, remote HTTP, HTTP full/admin, shell, cleanup execution, and agent/API execution outside approved stdio admin agent execution remain approval-bound.
MCP capability policy output must remain read-only and must not turn admin profile reporting into cleanup, provider/API, shell, daemon/session, credential, socket, or remote-listener authority.
Runtime must not expose arbitrary shell execution through CLI, review, or MCP adapter paths.
Codex plugin metadata must use the local stdio adapter and must not add external upload, profile reuse, or credential storage.
Resource status preflight must remain read-only and must not mutate host cache, swap, files, processes, profiles, or external services.
Resource guard must remain additive and must not change review findings, metrics, existing action plans, or release readiness.

Artifact cleanup must require explicit execute, stay under the configured artifact root, use plan hashes and candidate locks, write receipts, avoid directory deletion, and avoid MCP cleanup execution.
Agent advisory package, ingest, and report must remain local, advisory-only, free of provider API calls, free of credential storage, and separate from deterministic review gates.
Visual review result preparation must stay local, metadata-only, provider-disabled, transfer-disabled, and excluded from MCP execution.
Visual review execution must stay CLI-only, metadata/local-reference only, raw-pixel-transfer-disabled, credential-storage-free, and excluded from MCP execution.
Visual review dashboard must stay read-only, local-metadata-only, no-provider, no-raw-pixel, no-write, and gate-neutral while allowing safe MCP inspection.
Language settings must stay local, read-only, provider-free, no-translation-execution, no-write, no-browser, no-shell, and gate-neutral while allowing safe MCP inspection.
Localization resources and report templates must stay provider-free, preserve raw evidence and canonical enums, avoid file writes, and expose only read-only safe MCP inspection.
Translation readiness and dry-run must stay provider-free/no-network, avoid credential reads, preserve raw evidence, write no artifacts, and keep MCP translation execution unavailable.
MCP execution gate reporting must stay read-only and must not change MCP permissions, write artifacts, delete files, call providers, read credentials, read raw pixels, or expose execution tools.

Operation registry reporting must stay read-only and must not turn roadmap entries into execution permission, cleanup, capture, provider, translation, publication, migration, alias removal, or shell authority.

Operation roadmap reporting must stay read-only and must not promote draft roadmap entries into live execution permission, product-plan commitments, remote CI, publication, cleanup, capture, provider, translation, migration, alias removal, or shell authority.

Operation contracts reporting must stay read-only and must not issue execute tokens, enforce live gates, write receipts, run harnesses, broaden MCP permissions, or turn contract shapes into execution authority.

Operation policy reporting must stay read-only and must not issue tokens, write receipts from the report, run generic harnesses, broaden MCP permissions beyond approved stdio admin agent execution, or turn readiness output into execution authority.

Operation admin readiness reporting must stay read-only and must not issue execute tokens, run generic harnesses, broaden MCP permissions beyond approved stdio admin agent execution, or treat readiness as execution authority.

Operation provider readiness reporting must stay read-only and must not call providers, execute local runners, read credential values, transfer evidence from the report, expose provider execution beyond approved stdio admin agent execution, or treat readiness/status-list metadata as execution authority.

Agentic Human Review must stay CLI-only with non-executing proposals/readiness/quality diagnostics plus read-only evidence-set, human-baseline lifecycle, batch comparison, evaluator policy, xhigh mechanical effort enforcement, longitudinal quality, and claim-policy diagnostics; supported CLI runtime-result wrapper handling must not weaken raw advisory-result validation; synthetic/fake/injected/local pipeline evidence must stay excluded from proof and future claim numerators; real-provider proof-readiness must remain case-aware with calibration blockers, evidence-reference-backed benchmark records, no fabricated owner baselines, and strict claim-numerator exclusion when role/round/synthesis/placeholder/evidence-reference contracts are incomplete; Responses adapter benchmark repair must stay bounded, catalog-backed, and advisory-only; plan-hash/package-hash and exact transfer-flag enforcement, advisory-only output, generic agent execution bypass rejection, env-only API provider credentials, no raw provider response or credential storage, and no MCP exposure remain required.
Capture readiness must stay read-only and must not call OS capture APIs, load native capture dependencies, enumerate windows or processes, read raw pixels, write artifacts, or expose MCP execution.
Capture planning must stay read-only and must not capture pixels, call OS capture APIs, enumerate processes, write artifacts, call providers, transfer evidence, or expose MCP execution.
Capture handoff must stay workspace-confined and CLI/API-only, with no OS capture, artifact writes, provider calls, raw-pixel JSON, evidence transfer, or MCP tool exposure.
Desktop review provider-preparation planning must stay CLI/API-only, read-only, metadata-only, no-image-reread, no-provider, no-transfer, no-artifact-write, and excluded from MCP tools.
Desktop image review must verify capture handoff path/hash consistency while remaining no-OS-capture, no-surface-identity-claim, no-provider, no-transfer, and excluded from MCP execution.
Visual review aggregation must stay read-only, local-result-only, source-attributed, no-provider, no-raw-pixel, no-write, gate-neutral, and excluded from MCP tools.

Identity audit must stay read-only, local-metadata-only, no-Git-mutation, no-remote-contact, no-artifact-write, and must not authorize artifact-root migration, alias removal, npm publication, marketplace registration, or external evidence transfer.
Release readiness must stay local and no-publish, with no npm token reads, registry uploads, package identity changes, license changes, marketplace mutation, remote CI, or external upload.

Artifact-root policy and migration readiness must preserve legacy compatibility and must not run real migration, remove compatibility, delete outside the configured artifact root, or broaden MCP permissions.

Legacy alias removal readiness must preserve aliases and fail closed until explicit approval, with no package bin, MCP alias, plugin alias, artifact-root compatibility, or product-doc commitment removal.

Constrained shell readiness must stay plan-only and fail-closed, with no child-process APIs, shell interpreters, environment or credential value reads, file mutation, network access, free-form command text, or MCP shell execution.

Final hardening readiness must stay report-only, with no browser launch, MCP smoke execution, remote CI, Git mutation, package publication, provider call, shell execution, artifact migration, alias removal, or product-doc promotion.
